commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f2630cf11639042a9d5ca45fd60607513e1aec37)
commands.review_summary:
The branch routes command, status, health, export, cron, and TTS session-entry reads through the session accessor seam and expands the boundary guard for those migrated files.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +171, Other +22. Total +220 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction because this is a refactor PR. The merge blocker itself is high-confidence: live `check-dependencies` reports `src/config/sessions/store-read.ts` as an unexpected unused file, and source grep shows no production imports on the PR head.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can address the deadcode blocker by deleting or intentionally re-owning the stale helper while leaving the migration strategy for maintainer review.

target.title: refactor: route command session reads through seam
target.body:
## What

Path 3 / PR 3.1b cron/infra/commands slice for #88838. This routes cron, infra, and command session-entry read callers through the 3.1a session accessor seam while preserving the current file-backed runtime behavior.

## Why

This prepares the cron/infra/command read surface for the SQLite session-store foundation without flipping production storage. The disposable SQLite validation branch proved the migrated read callers can run against the SQLite foundation, and the remaining writer-store race is tracked separately as a 3.2 follow-up outside this PR.

## Changes

- Route command session reads through accessor
- Route status/export/health read paths
- Route cron/TTS/delivery read paths
- Preserve shared file-store semantics
- Add focused agent-scope coverage
- No storage flip or dual-read fallback
- No SQLite schema/store changes

## Testing

Source branch proof:
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/sessions.test.ts src/commands/sessions.default-agent-store.test.ts src/commands/sessions-tail.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts src/commands/status.test.ts src/commands/export-trajectory.test.ts src/commands/health.test.ts src/commands/health.snapshot.test.ts src/commands/sandbox-explain.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-session-target.test.ts` passed before the final agent-scope backport.
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/status.summary.test.ts src/commands/health.test.ts src/commands/health.snapshot.test.ts src/commands/status.test.ts` passed after source backport commit `ed3f914005`.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Local autoreview passed with no accepted/actionable findings.

Disposable SQLite validation proof:
- Branch: `integration/sqlite-flip-cron-infra-command`.
- Validation commits: `e5fc2ef023`, `5d04dd2a35`, `729b97bf18`.
- File-backed focused integration proof passed across sessions/status/export/health/sandbox/cron/infra tests.
- `OPENCLAW_SQLITE_SESSION_STORE_FOR_VALIDATION=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/sqlite-session-validation.test.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts` passed without the SQLite validation env.
- Integration `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...`, and `git diff --check` passed.

Source blockers: none.

External follow-up: `clawdbot-3b8` / `clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.17` tracks a 3.2 SQLite writer-store atomicity/idempotency race found during disposable branch review. That issue is outside this read-only cron/infra/command PR.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Command, cron, infra, health, status, and session-list read paths on PR #89122 route through the session accessor seam while preserving the current file-backed runtime behavior. This proof specifically covered the refreshed PR head after rebasing over #90463.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw gateway running from the live source checkout at ~/Projects/clawdbot, with the LaunchAgent gateway bound to 127.0.0.1:18789. PR head loaded detached at f2630cf11639042a9d5ca45fd60607513e1aec37, then rolled back to the original local main runtime at fc6d448138fc89b88693a058375f9da915578d52.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd ~/Projects/clawdbot

git fetch upstream main clawdbot-d9a/31b-cron-infra-command-seam
git switch --detach f2630cf11639042a9d5ca45fd60607513e1aec37
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway restart

node openclaw.mjs gateway status --deep
curl -sS --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:18789/health
node openclaw.mjs health --json
node openclaw.mjs status --json
node openclaw.mjs sessions --json
node openclaw.mjs cron status --json
node openclaw.mjs cron list --json
node openclaw.mjs sessions export-trajectory --session-key '<live-session-key>' --agent main --output pr89122-live-export --json
node scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.mjs

tail -n 300 /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-14.log > /tmp/pr89122-gateway-log-pr-tail.txt

git switch main
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway restart
node openclaw.mjs gateway status --deep
curl -sS --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:18789/health
node openclaw.mjs health --json
node openclaw.mjs status --json
node openclaw.mjs sessions --json
```

Evidence after fix: The PR runtime rebuilt and restarted the LaunchAgent gateway successfully. Gateway status reported runtime running, connectivity probe ok, and admin-capable. HTTP /health returned {"ok":true,"status":"live"}. health --json returned ok true with session summary keys present. status --json returned runtimeVersion 2026.6.2, sessionCount 16, and recentCount 10. sessions --json returned count 16, totalCount 16, and hasMore false. cron status --json returned enabled true. cron list --json returned 6 jobs. sessions export-trajectory wrote an 8-file redacted export for a live session. The local boundary guard printed "session accessor boundary guard passed." The sampled PR gateway log tail had 300 structured JSON log lines with 0 warn, 0 error, and 0 uncaught entries.

Observed result after fix: The command/cron/infra/status/health/session-list/export read surfaces exercised against the live gateway continued to work on PR head f2630cf11639042a9d5ca45fd60607513e1aec37, and the slice-specific boundary guard exercised the intended migrated-file ratchet. After the proof, the local gateway was rebuilt and restarted back on main at fc6d448138fc89b88693a058375f9da915578d52; rollback health/status/session checks were green.

What was not tested: No broad/full suite, Docker, cross-OS, or live external message-send path was run as part of this local proof. openclaw sessions tail was attempted and failed with TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim'), but the same failure reproduced after rollback on main at fc6d448138fc89b88693a058375f9da915578d52, so it was classified as pre-existing live behavior rather than a #89122 regression. The remaining PR check-dependencies CI failure is not addressed by this live proof.
target.author: jalehman

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
